protocol.question: For each pair: which render (left or right) more faithfully reproduces the SOURCE crop? Judge fidelity to the source: layout structure, typography scale/weight/color, spacing rhythm, component chrome (cards/buttons/borders), palette. Ignore: placeholder boxes standing in for photos/brand assets (BOTH arms use them by design), minor height differences, anti-aliasing.
protocol.gate: WITH-exemplars must win >=8/10 (ties do not count as wins)
protocol.separationOfDuties: The judge MUST NOT be the implementing agent and MUST NOT read answer-key.json or the falsifier HTML comments before verdicts are recorded. Arm identity is only in answer-key.json.
protocol.renderPath: WP-FREE: authored HTML rendered in local chromium pre-transpile (render-exemplar.mjs), per the P3 falsifier spec.

pairs.section: billing split: text col + pricing app mock
built-for-the-modern-web band (logos tail + heading + responsive-design card)
transitions band (easing panel + logical-props/container-queries + code panel)

notes: Authoring-arm honesty: both arms authored by the same agent under the same contract from the same crops; WITH arm additionally re-read the top-3 retrieved exemplars (retrieval-source-XX.json) and reused their conventions. Single-agent authoring means knowledge contamination is bounded but not zero — judging separation is the integrity boundary.
Pairs 09 and 10: the WITH arm shares the WITHOUT arm layout skeleton (deterministic restyle to exemplar conventions) — these pairs isolate convention transfer, not layout choice.
All 20 files lint-clean under eval/grader/atlas/lint-authoring.mjs; V-NEST flattens were applied IDENTICALLY to both arms (fairness preserved).
